prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: The Natural and Accelerated Evolution of EVA Adhesion Throug | Statement: At 10,000 hours accelerated aging, EVA adhesion falls to a level generally seen after long-term field exposures of more than 15 years.
2. [SILVER] Source: Influence of environmental conditions on dentin bond strengt | Statement: Dentin bond strengths of dental adhesive systems decreased with increasing relative humidity across six conditions.
3. [GOLD] Source: Influence of Lamination Conditions of EVA Encapsulation on P | Statement: Optimal gel content range for tested EVA materials was 84-90%, with samples below 70% showing low chemical/optical stability and weak adhesion.
4. [GOLD] Source: Influence of Lamination Conditions of EVA Encapsulation on P | Statement: Samples with gel content below 70% showed low chemical and optical stability, weak adhesion strength, and EVA flowing.
5. [SILVER] Source: Influence of Lamination Conditions of EVA Encapsulation on P | Statement: Laminates with gel content over 92% were more likely to yellow and showed less stable adhesion.
